text:
There was once a little town called Blissfield that had no troubles of any significance except for the eternal sadness that hung around its melancholy oak tree, known locally as Old Man Hawthorne.

One grey January morning, the only thing more pathetic than the bleak landscape was the lonely shadow that wandered the town—a melancholy creature with eyes full of regret.

That particular day, a young girl named Lily came stumbling down the cobblestone street. She was searching for something—a lost pet perhaps—or maybe just a place to rest her feet. As she approached the oak tree, she felt a cold breeze.

"What dreadful weather we've got," she whispered, her voice barely above the whispers of the wind.

The tree merely nodded—a gesture far more melancholic than anything it had to say—and sighed a deep, mournful sound.

Lily stopped, frozen. "Is it... is it goodbye?" she asked.

The tree tilted its head slightly. "Until another winter, dear."

With a shiver
